title: Add Parent Portal: Login, Dashboard, Child Progress View & Comment System

requirements.text: Add a 'Parent Account' section to the localStorage data layer: store parent accounts with fields { parentUsername, passwordHash, childUsername } where childUsername links to a registered student account. Add functions: createParentAccount(parentUsername, childPassword, childUsername), parentLogin(parentUsername, childPassword) that validates by matching childPassword against the linked student's stored password hash, getParentSession(), setParentSession(), clearParentSession(), and getLinkedChildData(parentUsername) that returns the child student's full profile and all associated data (subjects, chapters, notes, questions, mock tests with attempt history, flashcards with usage counts, question bank entries, planner tasks, targets, streaks, progress stats).

quotes: Parent will enter their by entering their username, son daughter's password (password saved by the student while registering)

acceptanceCriteria: Parent account creation stores parentUsername and childUsername with child's hashed password as credential
parentLogin() validates parentUsername + childPassword against the linked student's stored password
If childPassword does not match the linked student's password, login fails with an error message
getLinkedChildData() returns all student data namespaced under the child's username from localStorage
Parent session is stored separately from student session so both can coexist without collision

requirements.text: On the LoginPage, add two new buttons below the existing student login/register buttons: 'Login as Parent' and 'Create Parent Account'. 'Create Parent Account' opens a dialog/modal with fields: Parent Username, Child's Username (the registered student), and Child's Password (for verification). On submission, validate that the child username exists and the password matches, then create the parent account. 'Login as Parent' opens a dialog with fields: Parent Username and Child's Password, authenticates via parentLogin(), and on success redirects to the Parent Dashboard route.

quotes: In login page there is one more button login as parent or if first parent create a parent account

acceptanceCriteria: 'Login as Parent' and 'Create Parent Account' buttons are visible on the login page below the student auth options
'Create Parent Account' dialog collects Parent Username, Child's Username, and Child's Password
If child username does not exist or password is wrong, show a clear error message
On successful parent login, user is redirected to the Parent Dashboard page
Parent auth state is tracked separately from student auth state

requirements.text: Create a new Parent Dashboard page (route: /parent-dashboard). At the top of the page, display the parent's username prominently, and directly beneath it display the child's full name with a label like 'Viewing: [Child's Name]'. Throughout the page, all content labels must use personalized phrasing such as 'Your son/daughter [Name]'s subjects', 'Your son/daughter [Name]'s mock tests', etc., substituting the child's actual name and using 'son' or 'daughter' based on data available (default to 'child' if gender is not stored). The dashboard must include the following sections: (1) Overall Performance summary card showing chapter completion %, average mock test score, study streak, and targets met. (2) Subjects list showing all subjects with chapter progress bars. (3) Uploaded Notes count per subject/chapter with the ability to see note titles. (4) Questions added per chapter. (5) Mock Tests — list all mock tests with status 'Attempted' or 'Not Attempted', best score, and attempt count. (6) Flashcards — show total flashcard count and how many times each card has been reviewed/flipped. (7) Question Bank — list of all questions in the question bank filtered by subject/chapter. (8) A Comment Box section in a prominent center position on the dashboard with a label 'Send feedback to [Child's Name]' and three styled buttons: 'Scold 😠', 'Comment 💬', 'Appreciate 🌟' that pre-fill the textarea with a tone prefix (e.g., '⚠️ [Scold]: ', '💬 [Comment]: ', '🌟 [Appreciate]: '), plus a text area for the message and a Send button.

quotes: After login the parents can see their childs progress ,subject , uploaded notes, question, mock test made by the student given or not , flashcards how many times , question bank , students overall performance
At the top of this page , the user name means parent username and under it it's son name
There is a comment box in the middle of homepage or dashboard where written, (scold , comment, appreciate)

acceptanceCriteria: Parent dashboard route /parent-dashboard is accessible only when a parent session is active; redirects to login otherwise
Parent username is displayed at top of page; child's name is displayed directly beneath it
All section headings and descriptions use 'Your son/daughter [Name]' phrasing
Overall performance card shows chapter completion %, average mock test score, current streak, and targets met
Subjects section lists all child's subjects with chapter count and completion percentage
Mock Tests section shows each test with Attempted/Not Attempted status, attempt count, and best score
Flashcards section shows total count and per-card flip/review count
Question Bank section shows all questions grouped by subject/chapter
Comment box is prominently placed with Scold/Comment/Appreciate buttons that pre-fill textarea tone prefix
Send button is disabled when textarea is empty

requirements.text: Implement a notification system in localStorage: add functions addNotification(username, notification: { id, from, type: 'scold'|'comment'|'appreciate', message, timestamp, read: false }) and getNotifications(username), markNotificationRead(username, notificationId). When a parent sends a comment from the Parent Dashboard, call addNotification() targeting the child student's username. In the student-facing Layout (sidebar/header), add a notification bell icon that shows an unread count badge. Clicking it opens a dropdown/panel listing all notifications with sender ('From: [Parent Username]'), type badge (Scold/Comment/Appreciate with appropriate color), message text, and timestamp. Clicking a notification marks it as read and clears the badge count.

quotes: parent will write comment and send the message or comment will come to that student profile in notification bar

acceptanceCriteria: Sending a comment from the parent dashboard writes a notification entry to the child's localStorage namespace
Student Layout shows a bell icon in the header/sidebar with a red badge showing unread notification count
Badge disappears or shows 0 when all notifications are read
Notification panel lists each message with sender, type (Scold/Comment/Appreciate) badge, message body, and timestamp
Clicking a notification marks it read and removes it from the unread count
Appreciate notifications are styled in green, Comment in blue, Scold in red/orange
After sending a comment, the parent dashboard textarea is cleared and a success confirmation is shown

requirements.text: Add the Parent Dashboard route (/parent-dashboard) to the TanStack Router configuration in App.tsx. The route must be protected: if no parent session is active, redirect to /login. Include a logout option on the Parent Dashboard that clears the parent session and redirects to /login. Do not alter any existing student routes or auth guards.

quotes: So with your own logic do these changes in app. And no other changes

acceptanceCriteria: /parent-dashboard route exists and renders the Parent Dashboard page
Navigating to /parent-dashboard without a parent session redirects to /login
A logout button on the parent dashboard clears parent session and navigates to /login
All existing student routes remain unaffected

constraints: Do not modify any existing student authentication or session logic
Do not change any existing student-facing pages or routes
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Parent data must be stored in localStorage under a separate namespace from student data
No backend changes required — all parent portal data is read from the same localStorage used by students
nonGoals: No changes to the backend Motoko actor
No changes to any existing student pages (Dashboard, SubjectsPage, FlashcardsPage, etc.)
No real-time messaging or WebSocket communication
No gender selection field — default to 'child' if gender is not stored
No Internet Identity integration for parent login
No changes to the sidebar color or any other visual tweaks outside the parent portal
